mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly boosting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in the residential real estate sector, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between housing sales and starts shows no predictive power with a p-value greater than 0.1 in quarterly return models.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, generating positive short-term equity momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations where the industrial production index rises.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with rising unemployment creates stagflationary pressures that force aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with accuracy below 50% when both high inflation and rising unemployment conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth exceeding two percent confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in more than 65% of high-GDP-growth quarters, invalidating the earnings linkage.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect sustained labor market tightness and wage growth, supporting positive short-term equity performance over the next week.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns show no significant correlation with payroll changes, yielding a t-statistic below 1.5 across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a broad manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 70% of consecutive decline periods, contradicting the margin compression thesis.
